frame: substrate-centric (inverted): mass = convergence in substrate inflow, not a field source
absolute_time: tick is universal; c is the swim speed RELATIVE TO the flowing substrate; no clock varies
yukawa_dissolved: inflow is a 3D-flux power law (long-range); nothing emitted to attenuate
verdict: INVERTED FRAME, computed not asserted. (1) The GP inflow is DERIVED: a free-falling pattern carried by the flow self-advects with (u.grad)u = -GM/r^2 (ratio ~1.0 at all sampled r) — Newtonian gravity, equivalence principle for free (free-fall = floating in the river = force-free), and the profile u=sqrt(2GM/r) falls out, not fitted. (2) The inflow is u ~ r^-1/2 — a POWER LAW, LONG-RANGE: the Phase-3b Yukawa obstacle is DISSOLVED, because it was an artifact of the pattern-centric 'mass emits a field' frame; an inflow has nothing to emit/attenuate. (3) THE NUMBER: light swimming at c relative to the absolute-time pure inflow deflects with coefficient Delta_theta*b/(GM/c^2) -> 3.979 (b->inf extrapolation of a clean 1/sqrt(b) fit; GR=4, Newton/Soldner=2). THE PURE FLOW ALONE REPRODUCES FULL GR LIGHT BENDING. Why: the eikonal H=c|k|+k.u has effective g_tt = -(c^2 - u^2) = -c^2(1 - 2GM/c^2 r) = exactly Schwarzschild's time component — but it is MANUFACTURED BY THE FLOW's u^2 term, not by any clock slowing. So GR's 'gravitational time dilation' is here the INSTRUMENT EFFECT of being carried by the moving substrate; the absolute tick never changes. Absolute time + substrate-flow UNIFY: the flow generates the apparent time dilation (the framework's standing time-dilation-as-instrument bet), and full GR deflection follows. (4) RETRACTION: the Phase-3 instinct that one must ALSO slow c (a separate 'spatial piece') to reach 4 was itself pattern-centric and is REFUTED — adding c-slowing OVERSHOOTS: FLOW+slow gives 5.795 (alpha=1) and 7.479 (alpha=2). There is no separate piece to add; the flow's u^2 is the whole story. HONEST: not novel physics (river/Gullstrand-Painleve/analog-gravity is a known rewriting of GR). The value: the framework's OWN ontology (absolute time + substrate-centric + Intent drawn toward coherence) lands exactly on the flat-space, absolute-time, flowing-medium formulation of GR — dissolving the Yukawa obstacle, deriving the equivalence principle + GP profile, and recasting gravitational time dilation as the instrument effect it already claimed. The substrate-native open question: what flow does a coherent Intent convergence ACTUALLY produce from the substrate rules, and is it forced to the sqrt(2GM/r) profile? Bucket 0 unchanged (this reproduces GR, it does not beat it).
